new_terms.context: The Mac gross margin story is the clearest proof of Apple’s silicon thesis.
constrained by Intel’s $150+ processor ASPs and a roadmap that delivered clock speed regression instead of progress.
The M-series chips cost Apple an estimated $77 per unit in COGS (N3 wafers, packaging, yield loss)
Apple spent $6B (including the $1B Intel modem acquisition, 2,200 engineers, mask sets, and qualification costs) to get here.
Apple spent $6B (including the $1B Intel modem acquisition, 2,200 engineers, mask sets, and qualification costs) to get here.
Break-even volume is 1.3B units, roughly 5 years of iPhone production.
The N1 chip, Apple’s first in-house Wi-Fi 7 solution, replaces Broadcom’s $11 combo chip
Android OEMs still pay Qualcomm and MediaTek for chips Apple now makes itself.
